# Questions in Livestream 61

Live Stream #61: Understanding Hell, There Will Be A Test.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=573" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:33</a> | Why would a blemish in Leviticus 21:16-21 violate sacred space? | He answers that a holy God requires unblemished sacrifices and physically whole priests for temple service, so priestly defects disqualify ritual participation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=872" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:32</a> | If people in hell still have free will, can they change their mind and go to heaven? | He says no in the ordinary sense: their final disposition is fixed, though they may still choose actions that increase or lessen their suffering. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=1073" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:53</a> | In the Old Testament demons seem to participate in the divine council. Does that change, and if so why? | His view is that this ends at Christ's resurrection, when the demons lose standing in the divine council and move to opposing God's work on earth instead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=1283" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:23</a> | If Judges 1:1 is after Joshua's death, why is Joshua still alive in Judges 2:6-8? | He says Judges 2:6-8 inserts an earlier event as literary context, reminding readers what happened before the judges cycle unfolded. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=1645" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:25</a> | Do John 5:25 and 1 Peter 3:19 imply that Jesus preached to and saved souls in Sheol, including those on the bad side? | He says Christ preached grace to the saved, and may also have preached judgment to the condemned, but not salvation to the lost side of Sheol. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=1874" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:14</a> | When moving very heavy cargo, did ancient Egyptians use four-wheeled wagons or only two-wheeled carts? | He says wheeled carts appear late and rarely in Egypt; for the heaviest loads the Egyptians usually relied on sledges pulled by oxen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=2117" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:17</a> | Can you explain Daniel 9's seventy weeks, when they start, and how they end? | He ties the starting point to Cyrus's decree connected with rebuilding Jerusalem, placing the clock's beginning in 539 BC. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=2351" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:11</a> | Is there archaeological evidence for events in the book of Judges? | He says there is some, especially the Jerubbaal inscription and broader settlement evidence for Israelite occupation in the judges period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=2523" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:03</a> | Can biblical prophecy be intentionally ambiguous, with both near and far fulfillment at once? | Yes. He says this kind of double reference is common in ancient prophetic writing and gives Daniel 8 as an example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=2726" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:26</a> | Joel 3:6 says Jews were sold to the Greeks by Tyre, Sidon, and Philistia. When would that have happened, and did Jews even know the Greeks then? | He argues the text fits later Greek awareness and contact, especially once Greeks became more visible to the eastern Mediterranean world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=3079" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:19</a> | What is your opinion of Andrew Henry and Religion for Breakfast? | He says he does not have an opinion because he has not watched the channel, though he has received requests to review it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=3169" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:49</a> | Is an allegorical understanding of Adam and Eve acceptable? | He says some figurative and temple language is present, but he does not think a fully allegorical reading is the best understanding. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=3568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:28</a> | Did Israel borrow the pork taboo from Egyptian culture and then put it into Torah as Yahweh's command? | He says Egyptian and Israelite practice overlap here, but grounds Israel's food laws in Torah rather than reducing them to a simple borrowing claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=3868" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:28</a> | Did Akhenaten's monotheistic cult revive older Egyptian ideas about the rulership of Re/ Aten, or is that too speculative? | He says that reconstruction is too speculative and explicitly disagrees with Hoffmeier on that point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=4229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:29</a> | How much influence did Egyptians have on the Greeks, or Greeks on Egyptians? | He says the answer depends on period, but later Greek contact with Egypt clearly mattered, especially through trade, tourism, and intellectual exchange. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=4636" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:16</a> | How would First Temple Israelites have understood prophecy if it was not deterministic? | He says they treated prophecy as genuinely conditional, with outcomes responsive to human action rather than mechanically fixed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=5548" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:28</a> | Why did the Judahite monarchy keep an affinity with Egypt despite the Exodus and later warnings in Isaiah and Kings? | He says Egypt remained an attractive geopolitical counterweight to stronger empires, even though relying on it was repeatedly condemned. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=6099" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:39</a> | If Jacob was renamed Israel in Egypt, why say there were no Israelites in Egypt? | He answers that Jacob is called Israel, but the people were not yet identified as Israelites in the later covenantal sense and were labeled differently in Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=6775" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:55</a> | What are your thoughts on the discovery of General Wahibre-mery-Neith? | He says he only has the news brief and is waiting for more official detail, but notes the burial and many shabtis suggest a wealthy late 26th- or early 27th-dynasty figure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=6944" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:44</a> | What would a New Kingdom Egyptian know about Egypt's origins, unification wars, predynastic kings, and Khufu's pyramid? | He says they knew Khufu built the Great Pyramid and remembered unification, but predynastic royal memory had largely dropped out or become mythologized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=7467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:27</a> | What do you think of Simo Parpola, and have you read "The Assyrian Tree of Life: Tracing the Origins of Jewish Monotheism"? | He has not read the paper and cannot evaluate Parpola's argument directly, but says the tree of life motif is widespread and he grounds Jewish monotheism in Torah. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=7747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:07</a> | Who is your favorite guitarist? | He says he follows songs and guitar parts more than individual players, names music by Led Zeppelin, the Eagles, Nirvana, "Scarborough Fair," and says Bach adapts especially well to guitar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=8130" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:30</a> | Will you convert to Catholicism soon? | He says he does not disclose denominational leanings because he wants to avoid channel strife and preserve freedom to critique all denominations fairly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=8546" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:26</a> | Is the theory that the Sphinx is far older than usually dated credible, and is Graham Hancock a charlatan? | He rejects the old-Sphinx theory as crank material and calls Hancock an unreliable popularizer of fringe ideas. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=8738" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:38</a> | Why do we not have pyramid construction blueprints, and how were the stones cut and moved? | He says the Egyptians did not work to fantasy-level precision, used abrasion techniques to flatten stone, and that the documentary record simply has not preserved blueprints. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=9088" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:28</a> | Doesn't the Sphinx show rainwater erosion? | He says no: the visible pattern is overwhelmingly wind erosion, not rainfall erosion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=9363" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:03</a> | What is your favorite type of cheese? | He names a French cheese and explains that he developed a taste for it while living in Liverpool and buying marked-down cheeses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=9723" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:03</a> | If some pre-Exodus Hebrew names look Yahwistic, does that show Yahweh worship before the Exodus? | He says not necessarily, because names can be edited or retheophorized later and the evidence does not prove pre-Exodus Yahweh worship by itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=9976" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:16</a> | Did Egypt or Israel have anything like chess? | He says no exact equivalent existed; Egyptian senet is the closest comparison, but it was a different kind of game. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=10099" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:19</a> | How did the Israelites move the parts of the tabernacle? | He says the tabernacle was modular, built to be dismantled and carried in assigned sections by Levites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=10359" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:39</a> | Have you seen Galil's Facebook post about his readings of the tablets? | He says the readings look increasingly untenable to other scholars and describes the episode as a growing embarrassment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=10645" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:25</a> | What is your opinion of sand? | He says he likes sand, especially for excavation, and much prefers working in Egyptian sand to Israeli mud. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=10770" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:30</a> | What is your opinion of near-death experiences? | He says near-death experiences are not actual death, often involve immediate terror before later reinterpretation, and remain medically and theologically unresolved. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/llax2yc_y8w?t=11351" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:11</a> | Is it a sin for Christians to get tattoos? | He says not necessarily; the ancient ban was tied to ceremonial law and pagan dedication, so wisdom now depends on cultural context and conscience. |
